label: SES-031 v0.5 slice A build-execution closeout: 1 session, 0 decisions, 0 planning items, 8 references

session.title: v0.5 slice A — foundation infrastructure and dogfood migration
session.conversation_reference: Claude Code conversation against the slice prompt at PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/prompts/CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-v2-ui-v0.5-A-foundation-and-dogfood-migration.md. First of five v0.5 build slices. Single-commit session landing at 0933fc2. No transcript preserved per DEC-025.
session.topics_covered:
Seed prompt: PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/prompts/CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-v2-ui-v0.5-A-foundation-and-dogfood-migration.md. Eight categories of work executed against the v0.4 baseline (SES-024 closeout): meta DB infrastructure with its own Alembic chain at crmbuilder-v2/migrations/meta/ hosting the engagements registry table; per-engagement DB convention establishing crmbuilder-v2/data/engagements/{engagement_code}.db; two-database API server wiring with FastAPI dependency injection routing /engagements/* to the meta DB pool and everything else to the active engagement DB pool; ActiveEngagementContext singleton QObject with active_engagement_changed Qt signal mirroring v1's ActiveClientContext; lazy-migration helper run_engagement_migrations(engagement_code) applying the existing v0.4 Alembic chain to a named engagement DB; the eight-step dogfood migration module at crmbuilder-v2/src/crmbuilder_v2/migration/dogfood_v0_5.py (backup → meta DB → CRMBUILDER row → copy → verify row counts → delete v2.db → refresh snapshot → write current_engagement.json); the empty Engagements sidebar group container above Governance for slice C to populate; and the refresh-service file-watch entry for db-export/meta/engagements.json.

New modules created: access/meta_db.py (meta DB connection pool independent of the per-engagement DB pool); access/meta_models.py (EngagementRow ORM); access/engagement_models.py (Engagement dataclass + EngagementStatus enum, stubbed for slice B's repository); api/deps.py.meta_session dependency; migration/lazy_migration.py; migration/dogfood_v0_5.py; ui/active_engagement_context.py. Modified: api/main.py to apply meta Alembic and init the meta pool at startup with a new /engagements/healthcheck endpoint exercising the wiring; cli.run_api to trigger needs_migration() and run_dogfood_migration() at API startup with SystemExit(1) on failure and backup intact; ui/app.py to instantiate ActiveEngagementContext and load from disk; ui/sidebar.py to add the empty Engagements group container; ui/refresh.py to add the engagements snapshot to the file-watch map.

.gitignore extended with engagements.db, engagements/, current_engagement.json, v2.db.pre-v0.5-backup.

Test suite: 24 new tests added across four files — access/test_meta_db_connection.py (3), api/test_two_database_routing.py (3), migration/test_v0_5_dogfood_migration.py (7), ui/test_active_engagement_context.py (11). Full non-UI suite green at 546 passed, 1 skipped.

Dogfood migration ran against Doug's machine producing the post-state described in the slice purpose: v2.db backed up to .pre-v0.5-backup; meta DB created at engagements.db with the CRMBUILDER row; original v2.db copied to engagements/CRMBUILDER.db with all row counts verified; current_engagement.json points at CRMBUILDER. Application launches with CRMBUILDER as the active engagement and operates exactly as it did under v0.4. The slice does NOT yet wire the meta-DB bootstrap or migration trigger into the engine launchers — those gaps surface in the slice A follow-up (SES-030) which lands the bootstrap_meta_db() at API startup and the desktop-launcher dogfood migration trigger.
session.artifacts_produced:
Commit 0933fc2 on origin/main — v2: v0.5 slice A — foundation infrastructure and dogfood migration. Created crmbuilder-v2/migrations/meta/alembic.ini, env.py, versions/0001_create_engagements_table.py; crmbuilder-v2/src/crmbuilder_v2/access/{meta_db.py, meta_models.py, engagement_models.py}; crmbuilder-v2/src/crmbuilder_v2/api/deps.py meta_session dependency; crmbuilder-v2/src/crmbuilder_v2/migration/{lazy_migration.py, dogfood_v0_5.py}; crmbuilder-v2/src/crmbuilder_v2/ui/active_engagement_context.py. Modified crmbuilder-v2/src/crmbuilder_v2/api/main.py, cli.py, ui/{app.py, sidebar.py, refresh.py}; .gitignore. Added tests/crmbuilder_v2/{access/test_meta_db_connection.py, api/test_two_database_routing.py, migration/test_v0_5_dogfood_migration.py, ui/test_active_engagement_context.py}.

Dogfood migration executed locally: v2.db.pre-v0.5-backup created; engagements.db created (meta DB) with CRMBUILDER row inserted; engagements/CRMBUILDER.db created from v2.db copy; row counts verified to match (29 sessions / 86 decisions / 17 planning_items / 66 references etc. as of pre-migration baseline); original v2.db deleted; current_engagement.json written pointing at CRMBUILDER. db-export/meta/engagements.json regenerated.

Full v2 non-UI suite green at session end: 546 passed, 1 skipped (24 new tests added vs. v0.4 baseline). Lint clean across all touched files.

No PRD changes; no decisions or planning items written (build-execution session per DEC-014 records decisions already settled in SES-029); no version bump (those land in slice E).
session.in_flight_at_end:
Slice B (engagement schema + access layer + REST API) is next per the v0.5 implementation plan; will replace the slice-A stub healthcheck endpoint with the eight standard endpoints and flesh out the engagement dataclass and access-layer repository. Slice prompt at PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/prompts/CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-v2-ui-v0.5-B-engagement-schema-and-api.md.

Deferred gap surfaced during slice A execution but not addressed here: the migration trigger and meta DB bootstrap are wired into cli.run_api but not into the API process startup hook or the desktop launcher's first-launch path. A fresh install or any code path that spawns the API directly bypasses the trigger. The slice A follow-up prompt at CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-v2-ui-v0.5-A-followup-launcher-wiring.md was authored after slice A landed to close this gap; that follow-up executes as a separate Claude Code session (SES-030) outside the linear A→B→C→D→E build sequence.
